name: Hollywood Pawnbrokers / United Pawn
description_short: Hollywood Pawnbrokers offers collateral-based loans, buying, selling, and trading of personal items at their Vine Street location in Los Angeles with 36 years of experience.
description_long:
Hollywood Pawnbrokers, operating under the brand name "Pawn on Vine," is a long-established pawn shop located at 736 Vine Street in Hollywood, Los Angeles. The business has been operating for over 36 years, positioning itself as an experienced player in the collateral lending market in one of Los Angeles's most iconic neighborhoods.

The company offers a broad range of pawn-related services including lending money in exchange for personal property as collateral, buying items outright, selling merchandise from inventory, and trading items. According to their website, they accept "almost anything" as collateral or for purchase. Customers can use the shop to secure quick cash, store valuables securely while traveling, or liquidate personal items. The business operates Monday through Friday from 9:00 AM to 5:30 PM and Saturday from 10:00 AM to 4:30 PM, closed on Sundays.

What distinguishes Hollywood Pawnbrokers is their emphasis on security and organization of customer property. They explicitly advertise that they hold property in a "very secured place" and clearly mark all personal property to prevent loss or confusion. Their Hollywood location on Vine Street—adjacent to major entertainment industry infrastructure—may attract entertainment industry clientele and tourists needing quick liquidity. The company maintains two separate contact numbers and email addresses, suggesting either dual management or specific department divisions, though this is not clarified on their website.

As a pawn shop, this business serves a legitimate financial function for consumers needing immediate cash against collateral, though customers should understand that pawn loans typically carry higher interest rates and shorter terms than traditional bank loans. The lack of specific APR disclosure, loan terms, or fee structures on the website means customers must contact them directly for pricing information. No third-party reviews, complaints data, or regulatory compliance information is provided on the website.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: 36 years of operating experience in the Los Angeles market
Emphasizes secure storage and clear marking of customer property
Accepts a wide variety of items ("almost anything") for loans, sales, or trades
Convenient Hollywood location on Vine Street with accessible hours (weekdays 9 AM-5:30 PM, Saturday 10 AM-4:30 PM)
Multiple contact channels available (two phone numbers and two email addresses)
Offers flexibility: customers can pawn, sell outright, or trade items based on needs
Serves customers needing quick cash without credit checks
cons: No APR, interest rates, or fee schedule disclosed on website—customers must call for pricing
No loan term lengths, redemption periods, or default consequences specified
Limited online presence and no ability to check current inventory or apply online
No information about licensing, regulatory compliance, or third-party ratings
Pawn loans typically involve higher costs and shorter terms than bank loans, though this is industry-standard
best_for: Consumers needing emergency cash quickly without a credit check
People with valuable items who prefer collateral-based lending over unsecured personal loans
Tourists or travelers in Hollywood looking to secure valuables temporarily
Sellers of used items (jewelry, electronics, collectibles) seeking quick liquidation
diagnosis: Hollywood Pawnbrokers is best for consumers with valuable items who need immediate cash and are willing to use collateral-based lending. The primary caveat is the complete absence of rate, fee, and term transparency on their website—interested borrowers must contact them directly to understand the true cost of borrowing, which is essential before committing to any pawn transaction.
services: Collateral-based pawn loans (lending money against personal property)
Buying personal items outright for cash
Selling merchandise from inventory to customers
Trading items (exchange of customer goods for other merchandise or cash)
Secure storage of customer property while loans are active
Gold and jewelry evaluation (implied from pawn shop operations)
Electronics, collectibles, and general merchandise acceptance (implied from "almost anything" statement)
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
